purpose: synthesis/planning aid for negative, diagnostic-only, failed, and revise-classified findings; not new benchmark or paper evidence

classification_definitions.revise: The mechanism or hypothesis produced a result requiring design revision, not promotion.
classification_definitions.diagnostic_only: The result is informative for infrastructure or scope clarification but not benchmark-strength evidence.
classification_definitions.failed: The mechanism did not activate, produced an error, or was blocked.
classification_definitions.inconclusive: The test ran but the scenario, seed count, or horizon was insufficient to draw a conclusion.

failure_mode_definitions.mechanism_failed: The mechanism itself did not work as intended under the tested conditions.
failure_mode_definitions.scenario_too_weak: The scenario lacked the adversarial conditions needed to stress the mechanism.
failure_mode_definitions.evidence_diagnostic_only: The evidence is real but scoped to diagnostic validation, not behavioral claims.
failure_mode_definitions.infrastructure_only: Only the infrastructure or tooling was validated, not the hypothesis.
failure_mode_definitions.stale: The finding references artifacts or configs that are no longer current.
failure_mode_definitions.blocked: The finding is blocked by an unresolved dependency.

entries.hypothesis: Progress-gated topology reselection generalizes beyond the canonical h160 double-bottleneck slice and clears hard non-canonical slices.
entries.scenario: Three non-canonical hard slices (bottleneck_transfer, doorway_transfer, t_intersection_transfer) and one negative-control slice (simple_negative_control), thresholds 0.05/0.1/0.2m.
entries.why_failed_or_inconclusive: All 9 hard progress-gated rows ended horizon_exhausted (159 deadlock steps, 0 collision). The mechanism activates and generalizes diagnostically but does not clear any hard slice at h160. Negative-control rows succeeded cleanly (3/3 success, 0 topology switches).
entries.recommended_next_action: Design a successor targeting actual clearance or terminal-outcome movement. Stop same-family selector reruns on the canonical slice.
entries.claim_boundary: Diagnostic-only, not benchmark or paper evidence. Classification is revise, not promote.

entries.hypothesis: Observation noise (Gaussian position noise, missed detection, occlusion, delay) degrades planner behavior on a pedestrian-present scenario.
entries.scenario: classic_bottleneck_medium (stress_slice), seed 111, horizon 40, 1 pedestrian at ~14.9m closest approach.
entries.comparator: Baseline (no observation noise, ideal_state evidence class)
entries.why_failed_or_inconclusive: All progress, risk, and planner metrics are identical between baseline and perception-limited. The single pedestrian is too far from the robot to influence planner decisions; the planner is dominated by static obstacle clearance. Perturbation plumbing is confirmed working (occlusion masking active, 1 missed detection at step 15) but no behavioral degradation is measurable.
entries.recommended_next_action: Retest with a closer pedestrian, higher pedestrian count, or a scenario where the planner must actively avoid pedestrians.
entries.claim_boundary: Diagnostic-only, not benchmark or paper evidence. Confirms perturbation infrastructure works; does not measure behavioral degradation.

entries.hypothesis: A pedestrian-dominated live smoke fixture makes observation perturbations produce a same-seed planner behavior delta after the issue #2749 distant-pedestrian null.
entries.tested_artifact: hybrid_rule_v0_minimal step diagnostics with clean vs bounded-Gaussian/missed-detection pedestrian observations
entries.scenario: dense_pedestrian_stress live scenario matrix candidate, seed 2765, horizon 20. The live environment resolved 17 pedestrians but closest robot-pedestrian distance was 6.999m.
entries.comparator: Same-seed clean observation trace with ideal_state pedestrian observations
entries.why_failed_or_inconclusive: Perturbation plumbing activated (179 missed actor observations; observed actor count dropped from 17 to 5-12), but selected commands, progress/risk summary, collision flags, and closest-distance metrics were identical. The live scenario candidate did not satisfy the intended near-field pedestrian-dominated condition.
entries.recommended_next_action: Completed by follow-up #3233: the deterministic near-field fixture and same-seed clean-vs-perturbed comparison are recorded in docs/context/evidence/issue_3233_near_field_observation_noise/. Do not repeat the weak dense_stress live candidate for this claim boundary.
entries.claim_boundary: Diagnostic-only, not benchmark or paper evidence. Confirms live perturbation can change planner-input observations; does not show a planner behavior delta.

entries.hypothesis: The dissertation evidence ledger synthesizes all current thesis-area evidence into a claim-ready format.
entries.tested_artifact: Ledger rows for topology_guidance, signalized_behavior, observation_robustness, prediction, pedestrian_density_stress, and exported_tables.
entries.scenario: Full-repository evidence sweep across six thesis areas.
entries.why_failed_or_inconclusive: Five of seven ledger rows are diagnostic-tier or blocked evidence (topology, signalized behavior, prediction, pedestrian density, exported tables). One area (observation robustness) is release-backed but is a contract/provenance layer only. The exported-tables payload gap is resolved, but the Issue #3203 rerun remains invalid as benchmark-success evidence because PPO partial-failed and SNQI contract status failed. No row supports Results-chapter wording without qualification.
entries.recommended_next_action: Use the ledger as a planning aid to identify which thesis areas need new experiments before Results wording is possible. Do not cite any current row as benchmark-strength evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: Synthesis/planning aid only. Does not produce new benchmark evidence, paper-facing results, or safety claims.

entries.hypothesis: Richer predictive-planner maneuver authority (turn rate, action lattice, sequence-search depth, near-field geometry) closes the hard-case crossing-conflict success plateau.
entries.tested_artifact: Predictive planner authority variants (baseline, high_angular, dense_lattice, deep_sequence, nearfield_turn) plus single-knob ablations (nf_headings_only, nf_speedcap_only, nf_horizonboost_only) across 5 prediction checkpoints.
entries.scenario: predictive_hardcase_portfolio_v1 (cross_trap low/medium/high + group_crossing_high), broad robust seeds 200-229 over both seed halves, ~120 episodes/cell.
entries.why_failed_or_inconclusive: Only the near-field speed cap moved hard-success (~0.07 to ~0.10, about +0.03 absolute / +33% relative, consistent across checkpoints, ~120 episodes/cell). nf_headings_only, nf_horizonboost_only, high_angular, dense_lattice, deep_sequence, and combined_max_authority were inert (about baseline). The lift does not close the plateau (hard-success ~0.10) and checkpoint choice barely moved baseline, indicating the binding constraint is model/data-side rather than planner authority.
entries.recommended_next_action: Keep predictive_near_field_speed_cap as a minor safety-progress tuning knob, not a success driver. Stop further planner-authority tuning as a plateau fix; prioritize model-side bets (#3214 retraining, richer hard-case data) and proxy-vs-ADE selection (#3204).
entries.claim_boundary: Diagnostic-only, not benchmark or paper evidence. Small attributable speed-cap effect; classification diagnostic_only, not promote.

claim_boundaries: This register is a synthesis/planning aid. It does not produce new benchmark evidence, paper-facing results, or safety claims.
Every entry preserves the original diagnostic or negative classification. Entries are not promoted to stronger claims by inclusion in this register.
Fallback, degraded, diagnostic-only, proxy-only, unavailable, stale, or missing-denominator rows weaken or block claim wording.
